channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bantayog ng mga Bayani announces summer workshops for children's book illustration and writing
item.description: Bantayog ng mga Bayani is inviting aspiring young artists and writers to join its upcoming summer workshops this May, focusing on children’s book illustration and creative writing. These workshops aim...

item.title: Fadiel Adams granted bail of R10,000; due back in court in August
item.description: Fadiel Adams, the leader of the National Coloured Congress, was granted bail of R10,000 at Pinetown Magistrate's Court on Friday, bringing a sense of relief to his family who celebrated inside the cou...

item.title: South Africa bans highly toxic pesticide terbufos following child fatalities in Soweto
item.description: South Africa's Agriculture Minister, John Steenhuisen, has officially prohibited the sale, acquisition, use, and disposal of agricultural remedies containing terbufos, a pesticide linked to the deaths...

item.title: George Conway compares Roy Cohn's influence on Donald Trump to a mentor relationship
item.description: George Conway, former Republican insider and ex-husband of political commentator Kellyanne Conway, spoke in an interview with Katie Couric about the formative influence of Roy Cohn on Donald Trump. Co...

item.title: Kiharu MP Nyoro proposes legislative amendments to exempt fuel from VAT and cut pump prices
item.description: Kiharu Member of Parliament Ndindi Nyoro has formally notified the Clerk of the National Assembly of his intention to introduce legislative amendments aimed at significantly reducing fuel prices in Ke...

item.title: Indian Rupee Hits Record Low Amid Rising Oil Prices and West Asia Conflict
item.description: The Indian rupee has declined to a historic low, closing at 95.8 against the US dollar on Friday. During intraday trade, it even crossed the 96-per-dollar mark, influenced by rising global crude price...

item.title: RJ Scaringe secures $12 billion in funding across three ventures, continuing to attract investor interest
item.description: RJ Scaringe, the founder of Rivian and serial entrepreneur, has raised over $12.3 billion from venture capital, strategic, and institutional investors across three startups. His newest venture, Mind R...

item.title: Union Education Minister announces NEET-UG will switch to computer-based format from next year following paper leak
item.description: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan announced that the undergraduate National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET-UG) will be conducted in a computer-based format from next year. This decision...

item.title: Uganda invites Tanzanian investors to fund part of Malaba-Kampala SGR via €405 million Sukuk
item.description: Uganda is actively seeking Tanzanian investors to participate in its first sovereign Sukuk bond, valued at €405 million (around Sh1.2 trillion), aimed at partially funding the Malaba-Kampala Standard ...

item.title: Trump Calls New York Times Reporter Treasonous During Press Briefing
item.description: During a press briefing aboard Air Force One on May 15, 2026, President Donald Trump accused New York Times reporter David Sanger of committing treason following a question about the ongoing war with ...

item.title: David Beckham achieves billionaire status through football investments and fashion business
item.description: David Beckham, di former England football captain, don reach one big milestone: him don become Britain’s first billionaire sportsperson, according to di 2026 Sunday Times Rich List. Him and him wife, ...

item.title: Manitoba faces heightened wildfire risk as dry weather persists
item.description: Manitoba is experiencing an elevated risk of wildfires as dry conditions persist across the province. Officials are warning residents to remain vigilant, particularly in rural and forested areas, wher...

item.title: Labour Faces Crucial Leadership Choice Ahead of UK General Election
item.description: Labour is confronting a critical moment following its disappointing performance in the May elections across England, Scotland, and Wales. Jonathan Freedland argues that the party must select a leader ...

item.title: Investors urge Albanese government to restrict capital gains tax changes to property flipping only

item.title: Three Indigenous Tourism Organizations Withdraw from ITAC Over Financial and Governance Issues
item.description: Three provincial Indigenous tourism organizations — Indigenous Tourism Ontario (ITO), Indigenous Tourism B.C. (ITBC), and Nova Scotia Indigenous Tourism Enterprise Network (NSITEN) — have formally ann...

item.title: Punjab government allows markets and shops to remain open until further notice till June 1
item.description: LAHORE: The Punjab government has decided to temporarily lift the earlier restrictions on market and shop closing timings, responding to protests from traders, shopping mall associations, and the gene...

item.title: NEMA confirms website breach, urges public to ignore false information
item.description: The National Emergency Management Agency (NEMA) don alert say unauthorised persons don gain access to their official website. NEMA dey carry out all necessary technical actions to fix the breach, secu...

item.title: Meru Girl to Receive Urgent Treatment for Rare Reproductive Condition at KNH
item.description: Abigael Kendi, a 19-year-old girl from Meru, has suffered for over ten years due to a rare medical condition involving two vaginas and wombs, combined with endometriosis, which causes extremely painfu...
